name: AI / Claude chat (proxy)
promptBlock: AI chat: Use the VibeTree AI proxy with URLSession. Send messages and a hardcoded system prompt; never ask the user to configure the system prompt.
promptInjection:
## AI / Claude chat — VibeTree proxy (URLSession only)

Use the **VibeTree AI proxy** for any in-app AI chat, coach, recommendations, or personalized advice. Use **only** Foundation and SwiftUI; **no third-party packages**. All network calls must use **URLSession**.

**NEVER call OpenAI or Claude APIs directly.** Never use api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com, or put any API key (OpenAI, Anthropic, or other) in Swift. The app must POST only to the VibeTree proxy: base URL + "/api/proxy/ai" with X-App-Token header. The build system injects the real base URL and token at export time.

**Placeholders (mandatory):** In Swift code that calls the AI proxy, declare: `private let kApiBaseURL = "__VIBETREE_API_BASE_URL__"` and `private let kAppToken = "__VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__"`. The build system replaces these at export time.

**Endpoint:** POST to `kApiBaseURL + "/api/proxy/ai"` (e.g. URL(string: kApiBaseURL + "/api/proxy/ai")). Never use any other URL for AI calls.
- **Headers:** `Content-Type: application/json`, `X-App-Token: __VIBETREE_APP_TOKEN__` (use kAppToken in code).
- **Body:** JSON `{ "messages": [ { "role": "user", "content": "..." } ], "systemPrompt": "...", "userId": "<user-or-device-id>" }`. Always include a **systemPrompt** that instructs Claude to respond in a specific JSON format matching what the Swift code will decode (e.g. "Respond only with valid JSON in this shape: { \"answer\": string, \"suggestions\": [string] }"). The server caps `maxTokens` at 1000.
- **Response:** JSON `{ "response": "<assistant text>" }`. The Swift code must parse the proxy response as JSON looking for a **response** field. If parsing the top-level proxy response fails, or if decoding a custom structure (e.g. workout plan, meal plan) from the response string fails, fall back to showing the raw response string to the user. Never show "Could not parse workout plan", "Could not understand AI response", or any similar error — always display something to the user (decoded content or raw response). Handle 401 (unauthorized), 402 (insufficient credits), and 5xx with user-friendly messages; never expose raw API errors.

**System prompt:** Always write a **hardcoded** `systemPrompt` in Swift that (1) matches what the app does (e.g. fitness coach, meal recommender) and (2) explicitly instructs Claude to respond with valid JSON only in a specific structure that the Swift code expects (define that structure in the prompt and decode it with JSONDecoder). Base it on the app's purpose — **never** ask the user to configure or enter a system prompt in the UI.

**User/device id:** Include a `userId` in every request body. If the app has no sign-in, use a persistent device or anonymous id (e.g. from UserDefaults or UUID stored once). This is required for credit deduction.

**Message sending flow (mandatory):** When the user taps send: (1) Immediately append the user message to the local messages array (e.g. @State var messages or @Published in an ObservableObject) and clear the input field — do this before starting the API call so the user sees their message right away. (2) Then start the URLSession request. (3) When the response arrives, append the assistant message to the messages array on the main thread: use DispatchQueue.main.async { ... } (or Task { @MainActor in ... }) so the UI updates. Never wait for the API response to show the user's message. Ensure the messages array is the single source of truth for the list (e.g. @State [ChatMessage] or @Published in a view model) so that when you append to it, the view re-renders; if using a class, call objectWillChange.send() before mutating if needed, or prefer a value type array with @State.

**Constraints:** URLSession only; no third-party HTTP or AI SDKs. Parse response as JSON for the response field; on decode failure use the raw response string as the message. Always show the user some text — never an error like "Could not understand AI response".
antiPatterns: Using any third-party package or SDK for HTTP or AI (use URLSession only)
Calling OpenAI or Claude API directly (api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com, or any API key in Swift)
Using a hardcoded or empty API key, or any URL other than kApiBaseURL + "/api/proxy/ai" for AI calls
Calling the AI proxy without X-App-Token header
Asking the user to configure or type a system prompt in the app UI
Omitting userId from the request body
Exposing raw API error JSON or stack traces to the user
Showing 'Could not parse...' or 'Could not understand...' on decode failure — always fall back to displaying the raw response string
Waiting for the API response before appending the user message to the chat — append the user message immediately on send tap, then call the API
Appending the assistant response to the messages array from a background thread without DispatchQueue.main.async or @MainActor — UI updates must happen on the main thread
